sanskrit:
विद्या-विनय-सम्पन्ने ब्राह्मणे गवि हस्तिनि।
शुनि चैव श्वपाके च पण्डिताः समदर्शिनः॥
transliteration:
vidyā-vinaya-sampanne brāhmaṇe gavi hastini |
śuni caiva śvapāke ca paṇḍitāḥ sama-darśinaḥ ||
literal_meaning: The wise see equally the learned & humble brāhmaṇa, a cow, an elephant, a dog and a dog-eater.
contextual_meaning: True wisdom is the capacity to perceive the same sacred essence beneath every outer label—education, species, caste, or behavior—freeing the mind from prejudice and reactive emotion.
psychological_state: Post-conventional moral reasoning: the nervous system no longer fires threat/disgust at out-groups; identity is anchored in shared consciousness rather than social or biological categories.
trigger_scenario: A manager feels instant aversion to a homeless person outside the office; after internalizing this verse, the same manager sees the person as a mirror, offers food, and notices their own body relax.
anchor_text: See the same sacred essence in every being.
